title: Modify --user flag for podman create and run
body:
If an integer is passed into the --user flag, i.e --user=1234
don't look up the user in /etc/passwd, just assign the integer as the uid.

Fixes https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/issues/631

Signed-off-by: umohnani8 <umohnani@redhat.com>

comments.body: Hi! Ignoramus here, but could we take a fresh look at #631? In particular, take a step back and review if we really want docker compatibility here? I kind of think that running as an undefined user is an undefined corner case, and still think that podman's current action (blocking it) is preferable to docker's.

@edsantiago I think you should be allowed to pick a random UID to run a container and not require the UID be defined in the /etc/passwd.

This is what OpenShift is doing where it takes a container and runs it as the users UID.  

The problem with doing this in the past was a evil user could define an entry in /etc/passwd with 

3267:*:0:0

Which would fool the container runtime as to run the account as the root user.   This patch prevents that.
What I would like to see is if the admin specifies --uid 3267
and the etc/passwd has

```
dwalsh:x:3267:3267:Daniel J Walsh:/home/dwalsh:/bin/bash
```

That the process runs with GID=3267.  Currently in docker and podman this process would run as GID=0.

Then the third problem would be that no entry for UID==3267 exists, should it run as GID=0, I believe it should NOT, and that we should just set the GID=UID, which does not match Docker, but I think is a good compromise.
@rhatdan Tend to agree on setting GID=0 in that case - GID=UID makes more sense from a security standpoint

Okay modified the code so that GID == UID if UID does not exist in /etc/passwd. These are the different cases I tested:
```
➜  libpod git:(user) ✗ sudo podman run --rm --user 20 busybox id 
uid=20 gid=20
➜  libpod git:(user) ✗ sudo podman run --rm --user 8 busybox id
uid=8(mail) gid=8(mail)
➜  libpod git:(user) ✗ sudo podman run --rm --user 4 busybox id
uid=4(sync) gid=100(users)
➜  libpod git:(user) ✗ sudo podman run --rm --user sync busybox id
uid=4(sync) gid=100(users)
➜  libpod git:(user) ✗ sudo podman run --rm --user 20 --group-add wheel busybox id
uid=20 gid=20 groups=10(wheel)
➜  libpod git:(user) ✗ sudo podman run --rm --group-add wheel busybox id 
uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=10(wheel)
```
@rhatdan @edsantiago @mheon PTAL

@umohnani8 Looks like a nice group of tests to add, but I will merge this now.  Add tests in a new PR>

LGTM
